message:
chore(beads): 9ct8r scope investigation — refactor is multi-hour, release claim

Investigation on 9ct8r (pre-wipe atomic-swap gap) shows the minimal
'skip the pre-wipe' fix breaks resume: persist_lexical_rebuild_state_for_active_run_start
writes the state file to LIVE index_path, publish_staged_lexical_index
then moves that live path (with state file) into .lexical-publish-backups
and swaps the staged tmpdir in, so the new live has no state file.

The correct fix is a coordinated scratch-dir refactor of
rebuild_tantivy_from_db_with_options (950-line function, 25+
references to index_path) plus rebuild_tantivy_from_db_via_staged_shards
to route all writes through the scratch dir before the single
atomic swap. Detailed fix plan posted on the bead.

Releasing the claim so a pane with the multi-hour window can pick it up.
The ghw60 regression test still documents the current 0-doc carve-out
with a pointer to 9ct8r, so nothing silently regresses.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-9cby","title":"P4.1: Interactive TUI Wizard","description":"# Interactive TUI Wizard

**Parent Phase:** Phase 4: Wizard & Deployment
**Duration:** 3-4 days

## Goal

Build the interactive TUI wizard that guides users through export configuration, matching bv polished UX.

## Technical Approach

### New Module: src/pages/wizard.rs

WizardState struct tracks all configuration:
- agents: Vec<String>
- time_range: TimeRange
- workspaces: Vec<PathBuf>
- password: Option<String>
- generate_qr: bool
- title: String
- description: String
- target: DeployTarget (github/cloudflare/local)
- repo_name: Option<String>

### Wizard Steps

Step 1: Content Selection
- Multi-select for agents (with counts)
- Time range options (all, 30d, 90d, custom)
- Workspace selection

Step 2: Security Configuration
- Password input (with strength meter)
- Recovery secret toggle
- QR code generation toggle

Step 3: Site Configuration
- Title input
- Description input
- Metadata privacy option

Step 4: Deployment Target
- GitHub Pages / Cloudflare / Local
- Repository name
- Visibility (public/private)

Step 5: Pre-Publish Summary
- All settings reviewed
- Confirmation required

Step 6: Export Progress
- Multi-progress bars (dialoguer + indicatif)
- Filtering, indexing, encrypting, bundling phases

Step 7: Deployment
- Platform-specific deployment
- Success message with URL

### Rust Dependencies

dialoguer = "0.11"    # Interactive prompts
indicatif = "0.17"    # Progress bars
console = "0.15"      # Terminal styling

### Dynamic Content Stats

Query database for live statistics during wizard:
- Per-agent conversation counts
- Total message counts
- Time range of data

### Exit Criteria

1. All 7 steps navigable
2. Back navigation works
3. Validation on each step
4. Progress bars render correctly
5. Keyboard shortcuts work (Ctrl+C to cancel)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T01:38:10.155795Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T16:28:47.738599Z","closed_at":"2026-01-12T16:28:47.738599Z","close_reason":"Implemented full 7-step interactive TUI wizard with: dynamic agent loading from DB, workspace filtering, password strength indicator, recovery secret generation, encryption integration via EncryptionEngine, and progress bars. Wizard flow navigable end-to-end with validation and keyboard support.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-9cby","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-w3o7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:54Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-9ct8r","title":"[MEDIUM] rebuild pre-wipe exposes empty-index window to concurrent readers (atomic-swap claim doesnt hold end-to-end)","description":"DISCOVERED BY: commit 109560e5's atomic-swap publish contract cross-review via bead ghw60 (concurrent-reader crash-window test).

The atomic-swap publish path (src/indexer/mod.rs publish_staged_lexical_index at line ~10760) uses renameat2(RENAME_EXCHANGE) on Linux and a rename-based fallback elsewhere to swap a staged index into the live path without a reader-visible torn state. However, the surrounding rebuild pipeline does NOT respect this contract end-to-end.

In rebuild_tantivy_from_db_with_options (line ~11095-11107), when restart_from_zero is true (the common case for cass index --full / --force-rebuild), the live index directory is wiped via fs::remove_dir_all before the new index is even started:

    if restart_from_zero {
        fs::remove_dir_all(&index_path).with_context(...)?;
        fs::create_dir_all(&index_path).with_context(...)?;
    }

This leaves a multi-second window during which a concurrent reader observes either (a) a missing index path, or (b) an empty index (0 docs). The atomic-swap contract only covers the FINAL staged-to-live rename, not the pre-wipe.

EVIDENCE: tests/atomic_swap_publish_crash_window.rs::concurrent_reader_never_sees_half_torn_lexical_index_during_publish_swap observed ~52 consecutive polls returning 0 docs during a 2s cass index --full --force-rebuild run. The test currently accepts {stable_count, 0, None, Err} as legal to keep it green, with a comment pointing back to this bead.

IMPACT:
- cass search running concurrently with cass index --full --force-rebuild will return zero hits for up to several seconds while the live index is wiped and rebuilt. That regresses the user-visible 'search works while rebuild is in progress' promise the lifetime ledger implies.
- The documented 'atomic-swap publish' invariant on the commit message overstates what the production pipeline actually delivers.

PROPOSED FIX:
- Move the 'remove prior live' step INTO the atomic-swap publish logic so the live surface is only switched once, at commit-time, from old-good to new-good.
- Alternative: build the staged index into a scratch dir unconditionally (the staged-shards path already does this for the shard-farm rebuild), then have only publish_staged_lexical_index touch the live path. The non-staged rebuild_tantivy_from_db_with_options branch would need to also stage-then-swap, not mutate live in place.
- Either way, flip the test's legal-state set to {stable_count, None, Err} (drop the Some(0) carve-out) once the fix lands.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:04:26.407387177Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:04:26.407387177Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-9ct8r","title":"[MEDIUM] rebuild pre-wipe exposes empty-index window to concurrent readers (atomic-swap claim doesnt hold end-to-end)","description":"DISCOVERED BY: commit 109560e5's atomic-swap publish contract cross-review via bead ghw60 (concurrent-reader crash-window test).

The atomic-swap publish path (src/indexer/mod.rs publish_staged_lexical_index at line ~10760) uses renameat2(RENAME_EXCHANGE) on Linux and a rename-based fallback elsewhere to swap a staged index into the live path without a reader-visible torn state. However, the surrounding rebuild pipeline does NOT respect this contract end-to-end.

In rebuild_tantivy_from_db_with_options (line ~11095-11107), when restart_from_zero is true (the common case for cass index --full / --force-rebuild), the live index directory is wiped via fs::remove_dir_all before the new index is even started:

    if restart_from_zero {
        fs::remove_dir_all(&index_path).with_context(...)?;
        fs::create_dir_all(&index_path).with_context(...)?;
    }

This leaves a multi-second window during which a concurrent reader observes either (a) a missing index path, or (b) an empty index (0 docs). The atomic-swap contract only covers the FINAL staged-to-live rename, not the pre-wipe.

EVIDENCE: tests/atomic_swap_publish_crash_window.rs::concurrent_reader_never_sees_half_torn_lexical_index_during_publish_swap observed ~52 consecutive polls returning 0 docs during a 2s cass index --full --force-rebuild run. The test currently accepts {stable_count, 0, None, Err} as legal to keep it green, with a comment pointing back to this bead.

IMPACT:
- cass search running concurrently with cass index --full --force-rebuild will return zero hits for up to several seconds while the live index is wiped and rebuilt. That regresses the user-visible 'search works while rebuild is in progress' promise the lifetime ledger implies.
- The documented 'atomic-swap publish' invariant on the commit message overstates what the production pipeline actually delivers.

PROPOSED FIX:
- Move the 'remove prior live' step INTO the atomic-swap publish logic so the live surface is only switched once, at commit-time, from old-good to new-good.
- Alternative: build the staged index into a scratch dir unconditionally (the staged-shards path already does this for the shard-farm rebuild), then have only publish_staged_lexical_index touch the live path. The non-staged rebuild_tantivy_from_db_with_options branch would need to also stage-then-swap, not mutate live in place.
- Either way, flip the test's legal-state set to {stable_count, None, Err} (drop the Some(0) carve-out) once the fix lands.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:04:26.407387177Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:20:26.106601863Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":737,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-9ct8r","author":"cc_2","text":"2026-04-23: scoped investigation surfaces that the minimal "just skip the pre-wipe" fix is NOT safe without a coordinated refactor of rebuild_tantivy_from_db_with_options.

INVESTIGATION:

The 0-doc window comes from src/indexer/mod.rs:12322-12336:

    if restart_from_zero {
        fs::remove_dir_all(&index_path)?;
        fs::create_dir_all(&index_path)?;
        rebuild_state = LexicalRebuildState::new(...);
    }

This runs BEFORE the delegation to rebuild_tantivy_from_db_via_staged_shards at line 12507. The staged-shards path DOES build into a TempDirBuilder tmpdir and calls publish_staged_lexical_index at the end, so removing the pre-wipe would let publish atomically replace the old live index instead of the empty post-wipe stub.

HOWEVER, publish_staged_lexical_index does NOT carry forward the lexical-rebuild-state.json written earlier by persist_lexical_rebuild_state_for_active_run_start at line 11268 (inside rebuild_tantivy_from_db_via_staged_shards). That state is persisted to LIVE index_path, and when publish swaps the staged tmpdir in, the state file is moved with the OLD index into .lexical-publish-backups/. After publish the new index_path has NO state file, so the next cass run treats the (fully-rebuilt) index as restart_from_zero again.

Today the pre-wipe masks this by guaranteeing a fresh state file gets created inside the pre-wiped dir, matching the tmpdir's assumed layout. Removing the pre-wipe without also migrating persist_lexical_rebuild_state_for_active_run_start to write into the staged tmpdir (or rewriting after publish against the NEW live path) would break resume semantics for subsequent runs.

ALSO: the non-staged fall-through path at line 12534-12565 opens a TantivyIndex writer directly on index_path — so scratch-dir redirect requires routing 25+ uses of index_path through a local shadow variable AND making publish_staged_lexical_index the only path that touches the live path.

This is a multi-hour refactor, not a 60-minute fix.

PROPOSED FIX PLAN (for the next pane that picks this up):

1. Introduce a scratch_index_path allocation right after index_dir(data_dir):
     let scratch_index_path = TempDirBuilder::new()
         .prefix("cass-lexical-rebuild-scratch.")
         .tempdir_in(index_path.parent().unwrap_or(Path::new(".")))?;
2. Shadow index_path with scratch_index_path for the remainder of the function when restart_from_zero.
3. Remove the pre-wipe entirely — the scratch path is already empty.
4. Change the final publish (bottom of rebuild_tantivy_from_db_with_options AND rebuild_tantivy_from_db_via_staged_shards) to call publish_staged_lexical_index(&scratch_index_path, &original_index_path) before returning.
5. Also route persist_lexical_rebuild_state_for_active_run_start to write INTO the scratch dir so the state file survives the atomic swap.
6. Audit the 25 uses of &index_path inside rebuild_tantivy_from_db_with_options to ensure each targets scratch_index_path.
7. Audit the parallel rebuild_tantivy_from_db_via_staged_shards (which already uses a staging pattern) to ensure scratch_index_path composition is consistent.
8. Drop the Ok(Some(0)) carve-out in tests/atomic_swap_publish_crash_window.rs to enforce the stronger invariant.
9. Verify via: cargo test --test atomic_swap_publish_crash_window and the lexical rebuild integration test battery.

ESTIMATED SCOPE: 4-8 hours of focused work, including careful audit of 25+ index_path references inside a 950-line function and validation against every existing rebuild test.

INTERIM: the ghw60 regression test (tests/atomic_swap_publish_crash_window.rs) already documents the 0-doc window as a known gap with a pointer back to this bead. No production regression — the existing behavior is consistent with what cass has shipped for weeks. The concurrent-reader gap is theoretical for most users because cass CLI runs are typically one-at-a-time on a given data_dir.

Releasing the claim so a pane with the multi-hour window can pick it up.
","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:20:25Z"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-9dyf","title":"[Task] Opt 6.1: Audit current canonicalize_for_embedding implementation","description":"# Task: Audit Current canonicalize_for_embedding Implementation

## Objective

Before implementing streaming canonicalization, understand the current implementation and all allocation sites.

## From PLAN Section 4.3: Canonicalization Hotspot

Location: `canonicalize.rs:80-95`

```rust
pub fn canonicalize_for_embedding(text: &str) -> String {
    let normalized: String = text.nfc().collect();  // Allocation #1
    let stripped = strip_markdown_and_code(&normalized);  // Allocation #2
    let whitespace_normalized = normalize_whitespace(&stripped);  // Allocation #3
    let filtered = filter_low_signal(&whitespace_normalized);  // Allocation #4
    truncate_to_chars(&filtered, MAX_EMBED_CHARS)  // Allocation #5
}
```

## Benchmark Data

- `canonicalize_long_message`: **951 µs**
- Only affects index-time, not query-time

## Research Questions

1. **What does each function do?**
   - `text.nfc().collect()` - Unicode NFC normalization
   - `strip_markdown_and_code()` - Remove markdown/code blocks
   - `normalize_whitespace()` - Collapse whitespace runs
   - `filter_low_signal()` - Remove comments, dividers
   - `truncate_to_chars()` - Limit to MAX_EMBED_CHARS

2. **Which allocations are unavoidable?**
   - NFC requires full string (look-ahead for combining chars)
   - Others can potentially be streamed

3. **What is the input distribution?**
   - Average message length
   - Percentage with code blocks
   - Percentage with markdown

## Expected Deliverables

1. Full understanding of each transformation step
2. Memory profile showing allocation breakdown
3. Identification of streamable vs non-streamable steps
4. Implementation strategy for streaming version

## Files to Investigate

- `src/search/canonicalize.rs` (primary)
- Call sites that use `canonicalize_for_embedding`

## Validation

Research is complete when:
- [ ] Each function's behavior documented
- [ ] All allocation sites identified
- [ ] Streamable steps identified
- [ ] Implementation plan finalized","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T03:19:37.073013Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T03:40:07.810822Z","closed_at":"2026-01-10T03:40:07.810822Z","close_reason":"Duplicates - consolidated into 9tdq/0ym4/gngt/3ix9 chain","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-9et","title":"Detail pane: conversation timeline","description":"Render full conversation with role coloring, timestamps, search-term highlighting, and open-in-editor action.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-23T07:56:40.912248Z","updated_at":"2025-11-23T14:34:53.229562Z","closed_at":"2025-11-23T14:34:53.229562Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["detail","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-9et","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-6hx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:54Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-9fs8i","title":"Define a lightweight hot-path ConversationPacket projection for lexical, fingerprint, and budget accounting work","description":"BACKGROUND:
The existing ConversationPacket bead is directionally right, but the many-core indexing problem needs a more explicit hot-path projection: enough information to support fingerprinting, lexical slicing, provenance normalization, and byte-budget accounting without dragging every sink-specific or user-facing field through the tight loop.

GOAL:
Define the exact packet/projection shape that the many-core indexing pipeline will pass between producer, worker, and sink stages.

SCOPE:
- Separate canonical conversation identity and provenance from derived lexical/fingerprint slices and budget/accounting metadata.
- Define which fields are mandatory for lexical rebuild, which are optional or lazy, and which remain outside the hot path entirely.
- Ensure the projection can be built equivalently from raw scan output and canonical SQLite replay.
- Make packet versioning and diagnostics explicit so mixed-version bugs are explainable.

DONE WHEN:
The many-core pipeline has a precise, memory-conscious unit of work that later beads can implement against without re-litigating field shape, ownership, or budgeting semantics.","design":"DESIGN / JUSTIFICATION:
- The hot-path packet is not the full application object model; it is the minimum normalized unit that lets multiple sinks share work without re-normalizing the same content.
- Byte-budget fields must be first-class, not an afterthought, because responsiveness depends on bounding in-flight memory by content size as well as item count.
- Stable hashes, provenance normalization, and lexical slices belong in the contract because future memoization, sharding, and equivalence logic depend on them.
- Avoid prematurely baking in giant eagerly-owned payloads if a slice/view or staged field can preserve the same semantics with lower memory pressure.","acceptance_criteria":"ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- The packet contract explicitly covers identity, provenance, stable hash inputs, lexical slices, fingerprint inputs, and byte-budget accounting fields.
- Builders from raw scans and canonical replay produce equivalent packet semantics for the same logical conversation, with fixture tests proving that equivalence.
- Diagnostics make packet version mismatches and omitted-field fallbacks explicit enough for a future agent to debug mixed old/new-path runs without rereading the original plan.","notes":"LOCAL VALIDATION / FUTURE-SELF NOTES:
- Put the intended consumer stages directly in the bead comments/tests so future implementers know why each field exists.
- Reject accidental packet bloat early; once a bloated hot-path object spreads through the pipeline it is painful to claw back.
- This bead is a prerequisite for safe parallelization because the worker API boundary must be explicit before queues and shard planners can be trusted.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-19T20:59:39.914272945Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T23:37:03.760971607Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T23:37:03.719618670Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","indexing","packet","performance","streaming"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-9fs8i","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-19T21:15:08.265699732Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-9fs8i","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.31","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-19T21:05:08.762739157Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":584,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-9fs8i","author":"ubuntu","text":"POLISH ROUND 10:
- Clarified the packet contract quality bar: it should explicitly account for empty conversations, giant conversations, redacted or partially unavailable payloads, and version-mismatch diagnostics, not just the happy path.
- Required validation should include fixture or golden tests proving raw-scan versus canonical-replay equivalence for normal, sparse, huge, and provenance-weird inputs, with structured omission or fallback logs so future agents can see exactly why a field was absent or lazily derived.","created_at":"2026-04-19T21:15:30Z"},{"id":609,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-9fs8i","author":"ubuntu","text":"EXECUTION TODO CHECKLIST:
1. Locate the current authoritative rebuild hot-path data structure(s) and identify which fields are currently dragged through the startup/projection/fingerprint path.
2. Define the minimal packet/projection shape in code, including identity, provenance normalization, lexical slices, fingerprint inputs, and byte-budget accounting.
3. Add builders or adapters from canonical SQLite replay and any relevant raw-scan or replay source so the contract is executable rather than aspirational.
4. Thread the new projection through the earliest safe call sites without broad behavioral change yet.
5. Add focused unit tests for empty, sparse, huge, and provenance-weird conversations plus raw-vs-canonical equivalence.
6. Run targeted tests/checks and record outcomes before moving to the next bead.","created_at":"2026-04-19T21:34:41Z"},{"id":610,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-9fs8i","author":"ubuntu","text":"IMPLEMENTATION NOTE:
- Replaced the ad hoc lexical rebuild hot-path shape (conversation row + grouped messages + later doc-ref context recomputation) with an explicit `LexicalRebuildConversationPacket` contract in `src/indexer/mod.rs`.
- The packet now carries normalized identity, provenance, grouped lexical slices, exact message-count / message-byte budget accounting, packet version diagnostics, and last-message-id metadata.
- The streamed rebuild loop now constructs packets once in `finish_conversation!` and the batch flush path emits Tantivy prebuilt docs directly from packets instead of recomputing a separate context vector.
- Added packet contract tests covering canonical-vs-normalized equivalence for host-only remote provenance, empty conversations, missing canonical ids, and large payload byte accounting / created-at fallback behavior.
- Validation run for this bead: `cargo fmt --all`, `env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-optscan cargo test lexical_rebuild_packet_ --lib -- --nocapture`, `env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-optscan cargo test rebuild_tantivy_from_db_ --lib -- --nocapture`, `env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-optscan cargo check --all-targets`, `env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-optscan cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check`.","created_at":"2026-04-19T21:57:52Z"},{"id":617,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-9fs8i","author":"ubuntu","text":"2026-04-19 completion update:

Finished the remaining contract-hardening work for `9fs8i`.

What landed after the earlier packet refactor:
- Added an explicit zero-copy `LexicalRebuildPacketFingerprintInput` view over `LexicalRebuildConversationPacket` so the hot-path packet now names the exact fingerprint/stable-hash input surface instead of only implying it through lexical message rows.
- Extended the canonical-vs-normalized host-only-remote regression to compare both `semantic_view()` and `fingerprint_input()`, proving that raw-scan / normalized input and canonical SQLite replay now agree on the logical fingerprint contract as well as lexical projection semantics.
- The new fingerprint-input proof immediately exposed a real bug in my first draft: I had included `missing_conversation_id` in the fingerprint surface, which would have made canonical replay and normalized/raw-scan disagree despite representing the same logical conversation. I removed that storage-local field from the fingerprint contract so hashing/fingerprinting stays tied to logical conversation content and provenance rather than DB-assigned identity.
- Wired the fingerprint-input view into the live pipeline trace path (`lexical rebuild pipeline received prepared page`) so the contract is exercised in runtime diagnostics instead of sitting as dead code.

Validation passed on the final state:
- cargo fmt --all
- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-optscan cargo test lexical_rebuild_packet_matches_canonical_and_normalized_semantics_for_host_only_remote --lib -- --nocapture
- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-optscan cargo test lexical_rebuild_packet_producer_builds_lookup_and_source_context_internally --lib -- --nocapture
- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-optscan cargo test rebuild_tantivy_from_db_ --lib -- --nocapture
- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-optscan cargo check --all-targets
- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-optscan cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
- cargo fmt --check

This should satisfy the remaining acceptance gap around explicit fingerprint/stable-hash inputs and canonical-vs-normalized equivalence.","created_at":"2026-04-19T23:36:28Z"}]}
